title: Feed Discovery, URL Identity, Scope, and SSRF Boundary · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Autodiscovery Links
summary: Users can submit arbitrary URLs, discovery follows every alternate link, redirects change authority silently, and hostname checks occur only before DNS resolution. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go autodiscovery links increment in authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Admit only alternate links with supported RSS or Atom media types, resolve references against the response URL, and bound candidates.
Distinguish a direct feed URL from HTML autodiscovery and require an explicit bounded discovery policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Autodiscovery Links · Theory Feed Go Autodiscovery Links
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 9620 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded feed discovery, URL identity, SSRF decisions, and provenance; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
